6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials
7	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.ChangeOfRings
8	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth
9	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Proper
10	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Geometrically.Irreducible
11	
12	/-!
13	# Cotangent space at the identity of a group scheme
14	
15	The cotangent space at the identity of a smooth proper geometrically
16	irreducible group scheme `G` over a base field `k`, defined as a
17	`k`-vector space. Mathematically, this is the pullback of the relative
18	cotangent sheaf `Ω_{G/k}` along the identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`;
19	the iter-130 Lean realisation (Replacement (B), per
20	`analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`) computes it as the base change to
21	`k` of the algebraic Kähler module `Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` on a
22	smooth affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around the identity-section image.
23	
24	This is piece (i.a) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile (see
25	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § "Piece (i): sub-lemma
26	decomposition for iter-128+ build" and STRATEGY.md § M2.body-pile).
27	
28	The companion rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (rank =
29	relative dimension `n` from `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`) is
30	deferred to iter-132+ for blueprint-RHS-pinning work; not in this file.
31	
32	## Status (iter-131 fix-up: pure-term body refactor)
33	
34	`AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is fully constructed
35	(no `sorry`). The iter-128 body (presheaf evaluated at the top open then
36	extended scalars to `k`) was found in iter-129 to compute the zero
37	`k`-module for every `G` in the target class with `n ≥ 1` (`Spec` of a
38	field is a single point, so the pulled-back presheaf collapses to `k`
39	and the Kähler module of the identity `k → k` is zero). The iter-130
40	fix-up replaced it with **Replacement (B): affine-chart base change** —
41	extract an affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around the identity-section image
42	on which the algebraic Kähler module is free of rank `n` (via
43	`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`), then base-change to `k` along the
44	identity section restricted to `V`. The iter-130 realisation passed
45	through `Classical.choice` to bridge `Prop` and `Type`, which left the
46	elaborated body with `Classical.choice` as its outermost head symbol —
47	opaque to downstream `unfold`/`whnf`. The **iter-131 fix-up** refactors
48	the body to a pure-term `noncomputable def`: `let`-bindings of
49	`Classical.choose`/`.choose_spec` on the `smooth_locally_free_omega`
50	existential expose `U, V, e, ψV` as named-but-opaque accessors, while
51	the outer expression is the explicit
52	`(ModuleCat.extendScalars ψV.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of … Ω[…])`. The
53	signature, set in iter-129 (free `{n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`
54	binder), is preserved. The companion rank lemma
55	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` lives in a follow-up declaration
56	(not in this file); the iter-131 body shape is the testable deliverable
57	witnessed by `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` below.
58	
59	## References
60	
61	- Blueprint chapter: `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § "Piece (i)".
62	- Analogist source-of-truth: `analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`
63	  (Replacement (B) construction; iter-130 prover lane closure chain).
64	- Project infrastructure consumed:
65	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`
66	  (in `AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials`).
67	- Mathlib `GrpObj` API: `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_`,
68	  `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth`.
69	- Mathlib Kähler / standard-smooth pieces (consumed via
70	  `smooth_locally_free_omega`):
71	  `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`,
72	  `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential`.
73	-/
74	
75	set_option autoImplicit false
76	
77	universe u
78	
79	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
80	open TopologicalSpace Opposite
81	
82	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj
83	
84	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
85	
86	/-- The **cotangent space at the identity** of a smooth proper geometrically
87	irreducible group scheme `G` over `k`, as a `k`-vector space (returned as
88	`ModuleCat k`).
89	
90	Mathematically: the base change to `k` of the algebraic Kähler module
91	`Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` of a smooth affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around
92	the identity-section image. By the forward Jacobian criterion
93	(`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`, consuming
94	`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`),
95	this Kähler module is free of rank `n` over `Γ(G, V)`, hence its base
96	change is a finitely-generated free `k`-module of rank `n`; the
97	iter-132+ companion rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (in
98	a follow-up declaration) pins the rank to `n` from the
99	`[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]` instance.
100	
101	The bracket / Lie-algebra structure is NOT packaged here — only the
102	underlying `k`-module is needed for the rigidity argument. Downstream
103	consumers that need the dual tangent space `𝔤` may take
104	`Module.Dual k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G)`.
105	
106	**Construction (iter-130 prover lane; Replacement (B), affine-chart base
107	change).** The body is built as follows:
108	1. The identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G` of the `GrpObj` structure gives
109	   a scheme morphism `ηleft : Spec k ⟶ G.left` (using the definitional
110	   identification `(𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))).left = Spec (.of k)`).
111	2. Take `x₀ := ηleft default ∈ G.left`, the image of the unique point of
112	   `Spec (.of k)` (which is a singleton because `k` is a field, so
113	   `PrimeSpectrum k` is `Unique`).
114	3. By `Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega` (forward Jacobian criterion,
115	   project lemma in `AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials`), there exists an
116	   affine open `V ⊆ G.left` containing `x₀`, an affine open `U ⊆ Spec k`
117	   with `V ≤ G.hom⁻¹U`, and the `appLE` algebra
118	   `Γ(Spec k, U) → Γ(G, V)` exhibits `Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` as a
119	   free `Γ(G, V)`-module of rank `n`.
120	4. Restricting `ηleft` to `V` yields a ring map
121	   `ηleft.appLE V ⊤ _ : Γ(G, V) → Γ(Spec k, ⊤)`; composing with the
122	   canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≅ k` (`Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) gives
123	   `ψV : Γ(G, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k`.
124	5. Extension of scalars from `Γ(G, V)` to `k` along `ψV` of the algebraic
125	   Kähler module gives the cotangent space at the identity as a
126	   `k`-module.
127	
128	**Caveat on canonicity.** The chart `V` used in the body is extracted
129	via `Classical.choose` from `smooth_locally_free_omega`'s existential.
130	The resulting `k`-module is therefore non-canonical *as a value*. What
131	the iter-131 body refactor guarantees is that the body's **structural
132	shape** — a base change to `k` of the chart-level Kähler module — is
133	exposed to downstream consumers: after `unfold cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`
134	and delta-reduction of the `let`-bindings, the outer head symbol is
135	`(ModuleCat.extendScalars _).obj (ModuleCat.of _ Ω[_ ⁄ _])`. This is
136	formalized as `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` below, which
137	the iter-132+ rank lemma can rewrite against. For the live consumer
138	(rigidity over `k`, via `rigidity_over_kbar`), the existence of a
139	rank-`n` free `k`-module is the only structural content needed, so
140	chart-canonicity is not load-bearing. A canonical (chart-independent)
141	presentation via the stalk-side cotangent `𝔪 / 𝔪²` is the content of
142	the blueprint's `lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge` (deferred to iter-130+).
143	
144	This compiles to a `ModuleCat k` with no `sorry`. The structural
145	properties (`Module.Free k`, `Module.Finite k`, rank `= n`) are content
146	for the iter-132+ rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq`; the
147	structural-shape accessibility needed by that lemma is witnessed here by
148	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars`. -/
149	noncomputable def cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
150	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom] [IsProper G.hom]
151	    [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
152	    ModuleCat k :=
153	  -- Identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`; on schemes this is `Spec k ⟶ G.left`.
154	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
155	  -- Image in `G.left` of the unique point of `Spec (.of k)` (`Spec` of a field).
156	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
157	  -- `Classical.choose`-chain through the `Prop`-level existential output of
158	  -- `smooth_locally_free_omega`. Each `let` names one component (chart `U`,
159	  -- chart `V`, the appLE inclusion `e`, and `hxV : x₀ ∈ V`) so the outer
160	  -- expression below stays in a pure-term shape — no `Classical.choice`

160	  -- expression below stays in a pure-term shape — no `Classical.choice`
161	  -- wrapper at the top level (iter-131 body-shape refactor).
162	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
163	  let U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose
164	  let h₁ := h.choose_spec
165	  let V : G.left.Opens := h₁.choose
166	  let h₂ := h₁.choose_spec
167	  let e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U := h₂.choose
168	  let hxV : x₀ ∈ V := h₂.choose_spec.1
169	  -- The pre-image of `V` under `ηleft` is all of `Spec (.of k)`, since
170	  -- `Spec` of a field has a unique point and that point lies in `V`.
171	  let htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤ ηleft ⁻¹ᵁ V := fun s _ => by
172	    rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage, show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
173	    exact hxV
174	  -- Ring map `ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ k` from the identity section restricted to
175	  -- `V`, composed with the canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≅ k`.
176	  let ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k :=
177	    ηleft.appLE V ⊤ htop ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom
178	  -- Equip `Γ(G.left, V)` with the `Γ(Spec k, U)`-algebra structure from
179	  -- `appLE` on `G.hom`.
180	  letI : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
181	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
182	  -- Body: base-change the algebraic Kähler module `Ω[Γ(G.left, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]`
183	  -- from `Γ(G.left, V)` to `k` along `ψV`. By `smooth_locally_free_omega`
184	  -- (consuming `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`)
185	  -- this Kähler module is free of rank `n` over `Γ(G.left, V)`, hence its
186	  -- base change is free of rank `n` over `k`.
187	  (ModuleCat.extendScalars ψV.hom).obj
188	    (ModuleCat.of Γ(G.left, V) Ω[Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])
189	
190	/-- Defensive lemma: `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G` is definitionally equal to its
191	explicit chart-base-changed Kähler module form. The chart `V`, the algebra
192	structure, and the Kähler module are opaque (`Classical.choose`-extracted) but
193	the outer `extendScalars`/`ModuleCat.of`-of-`Ω` form is exposed. This proves
194	that the iter-131 body refactor closed the iter-130 opacity defect: a
195	downstream rank lemma can `simp only [cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars]`
196	(combined with a `Classical.choose`-chain `obtain` on the same existential)
197	to rewrite the body to the explicit form. -/
198	theorem cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
199	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
200	    [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
201	    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) (V : G.left.Opens) (e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U)
202	        (htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤
203	          (CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]) ⁻¹ᵁ V),
204	      letI : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
205	        (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
206	      (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (n := n) G : ModuleCat k) =
207	        (ModuleCat.extendScalars
208	          ((CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]).appLE V ⊤ htop ≫
209	            (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom).hom).obj
210	          (ModuleCat.of Γ(G.left, V) Ω[Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec (.of k), U)]) := by
211	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
212	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
213	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
214	  let hxV : x₀ ∈ h.choose_spec.choose := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
215	  refine ⟨h.choose, h.choose_spec.choose, h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose,
216	    fun s _ => ?_, rfl⟩
217	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) s ∈ h.choose_spec.choose
218	  rw [show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
219	  exact hxV
220	
221	/-- **Rank lemma for the cotangent space at the identity.**
222	
223	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme `G` over `k` of
224	relative dimension `n`, the `k`-vector space `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G` has
225	`finrank` equal to `n`.
226	
227	**Proof strategy (per `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` §
228	`lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank`, Steps 1+2 live closure path).**
229	
230	* **Step 1 (chart-side Kähler rank).** Reproduce the iter-131 body's
231	  `Classical.choose`-chain on `Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega` to extract
232	  the same chart witnesses `U`, `V`, `e` plus `hfree : Module.Free Γ(G, V)
233	  Ω[Γ(G, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec k, U)]` and `hrank : Module.rank ... = n`. Apply
234	  `Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank` to get
235	  `Module.finrank Γ(G, V) Ω[…] = n`.
236	
237	* **Step 2 (base-change preserves finrank).** The body of
238	  `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is by construction the `extendScalars ψ_V`
239	  base change of `Ω[Γ(G, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec k, U)]` from `Γ(G, V)` to `k`. Apply
240	  `Module.finrank_baseChange` to push `Module.finrank Γ(G, V) Ω[…]` to
241	  `Module.finrank k (k ⊗_{Γ(G,V)} Ω[…])`, discharging the `Module.Free`
242	  hypothesis via `hfree` and the `Nontrivial Γ(G, V)` hypothesis from the
243	  existence of the ring map `ψ_V : Γ(G, V) → k` into the field `k`. -/
244	theorem cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
245	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
246	    [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
247	    Module.finrank k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (n := n) G) = n := by
248	  -- Reproduce the same `Classical.choose`-chain as the body of `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`.
249	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
250	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
251	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
252	  set U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose with hU_def
253	  set V : G.left.Opens := h.choose_spec.choose with hV_def
254	  set e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose with he_def
255	  have hxV : x₀ ∈ V := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
256	  -- Algebra structure on `Γ(G.left, V)` over `Γ(Spec k, U)` (matches the body's `letI`).
257	  letI algGV : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
258	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
259	  -- Freeness + rank witnesses from the existential.
260	  have hfree : Module.Free ↥Γ(G.left, V) Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)] :=
261	    h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.1
262	  have hrank : Module.rank ↥Γ(G.left, V) Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)] = (n : Cardinal) :=
263	    h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.2
264	  -- ψV ring map (matches the body's `ψV`).
265	  have htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤ ηleft ⁻¹ᵁ V := fun s _ => by
266	    rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage, show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
267	    exact hxV
268	  let ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k :=
269	    ηleft.appLE V ⊤ htop ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom
270	  letI algGVk : Algebra ↥Γ(G.left, V) k := ψV.hom.toAlgebra
271	  -- The Γ(G, V) algebra is Nontrivial because it maps to the field `k`.
272	  haveI : Nontrivial ↥Γ(G.left, V) := ψV.hom.domain_nontrivial
273	  -- The body of `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is `(extendScalars ψV.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of … Ω[…])`,
274	  -- whose carrier is `TensorProduct Γ(G, V) k Ω[…]` (under `algGVk`). Definitional equality
275	  -- of the carriers reduces the goal to `Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ...) = n`.
276	  change Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ↥Γ(G.left, V) k
277	      (Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])) = n
278	  -- Step 2: Base-change preserves finrank.
279	  rw [Module.finrank_baseChange (R := k) (S := ↥Γ(G.left, V))
280	        (M' := Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])]
281	  -- Step 1: Apply rank=n ⇒ finrank=n.
282	  exact Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank
283	
284	end AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj

255	#### Route A — Picard scheme via FGA
256	
257	Hilbert schemes, Quot schemes, representability of the Picard
258	functor for smooth proper geometrically connected curves,
259	identity-component construction.
260	
261	**Top-3 gating Mathlib pieces** (each is itself a multi-thousand-LOC
262	contribution candidate):
263	
264	1. **Hilbert scheme representability for projective schemes**:
265	   `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Hilbert.Representability` (doesn't
266	   exist). Decomposes into (a) the Hilbert functor as a functor
267	   $(\Sch/k)^{\mathrm{op}} \to \Sets$, (b) the representability theorem
268	   via Grothendieck's flattening stratification, (c) the
269	   smoothness/properness of the Hilbert scheme on smooth projective
270	   bases.
271	2. **Quot scheme representability**: representability of the Quot
272	   functor of coherent quotients on a proper scheme. Generalises
273	   Hilbert; depends on coherent-sheaf-of-finite-type infrastructure
274	   that is partially in Mathlib.
275	3. **Identity-component construction `G^0 ⊆ G`**: for a $k$-group
276	   scheme `G` locally of finite type, the connected component of the
277	   identity as a closed (and open) subgroup scheme. Requires
278	   `IsConnectedSpace` for the identity component, plus the topological
279	   connectedness of identity components of group objects in `Scheme/k`.
280	
281	#### Route B — Symmetric powers + Stein factorisation
282	
283	Scheme-level symmetric powers, finite-group-scheme quotients,
284	Brill–Noether–Riemann–Roch, Stein factorisation.
285	
286	**Top-3 gating Mathlib pieces**:
287	
288	1. **Symmetric powers of schemes `Sym^n X` with smoothness**:
289	   `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.SymmetricPower` (doesn't exist).
290	   Requires the finite-group-quotient construction `X^n / S_n` with
291	   smoothness when `X` is smooth (Fogarty's symmetric-product
292	   computation).
293	2. **Stein factorisation theorem**: for a proper morphism
294	   `f : X → Y` of locally Noetherian schemes, $f_* \mathcal O_X$ is a
295	   coherent $\mathcal O_Y$-algebra and $f$ factors as
296	   $X \to \mathrm{Spec}_Y(f_*\mathcal O_X) \to Y$. Requires coherent
297	   $\mathcal O$-module cohomology of proper morphisms (partially in
298	   Mathlib).
299	3. **Brill–Noether and Riemann–Roch**: as for M2.d, the curve-side
300	   Riemann–Roch input is the largest Mathlib gap; absent from
301	   Mathlib in usable form for the project's genus-via-Ext
302	   definition.
303	
304	#### Route-pick decision criterion
305	
306	**Trigger** (revised iter-122 per strategy-critic-iter122). The
307	Mathlib-coverage audit is an offline LOC estimation that does NOT
308	depend on M1 closure. **Schedule: iter-123** (immediately after the
309	iter-122 M1 work returns its first results, regardless of whether
310	M1 closes; the audit is a parallel deliverable, not a sequential
311	gate). The route is "picked" once the audit returns its honest LOC
312	totals.
313	
314	**Criterion.** Run a Mathlib coverage audit of each route's top-3
315	gating pieces. For each missing piece, estimate the project-internal
316	LOC required to build it (the typical multi-K LOC contribution). Pick
317	the route with the smaller cumulative LOC estimate. **Tiebreaker**:
318	if estimates are within 20% of each other, prefer the route whose
319	top-3 gating pieces have the most cross-utility outside the Jacobian
320	arc (the Hilbert/Quot/representability infrastructure of Route A has
321	broader downstream value; the symmetric-power / Stein machinery of
322	Route B is more curve-specific). **Hard fallback**: if both routes
323	exceed 5000 LOC of estimated upstream-Mathlib work, escalate to the
324	user for an external-PR routing decision (likely: post an upstream
325	PR for at least the smallest gating piece and continue on the
326	remaining routes-and-arms while waiting).
327	
328	#### Per-iter progress signal for M3
329	
330	During M3's multi-iter run, the progress signal is:
331	
332	- **Iter $N$ "on track" criteria**:
333	  1. At least one named gating piece has its scaffolding declaration
334	     (with `sorry` body) introduced or its body partially closed.
335	  2. The cumulative LOC against the route's bill-of-materials
336	     decreases or stays flat (an iter that ADDS LOC to the BOM is
337	     CHURNING; the route is on a deeper gap than estimated).
338	  3. The cumulative `\leanok`-tagged declarations across the BOM
339	     increases by at least one per 2 iters (otherwise CHURNING).
340	- **Iter "off track" signal**:
341	  1. Two iters in a row introduce a new helper declaration whose
342	     blueprint chapter doesn't yet exist (blueprint expansion is
343	     overdue).
344	  2. The prover lane on the BOM's smallest piece returns INCOMPLETE
345	     twice consecutively (the piece is too tightly coupled to
346	     Mathlib infrastructure; pivot strategy or escalate to user).

380	## Soundness rules
381	
382	- **No new axioms.** Every closed declaration must `lean_verify` to
383	  kernel-only axioms (`propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound`). The
384	  iter-122 strategy-critic raised an alternative end-state of
385	  promoting `nonempty_jacobianWitness` to a named project `axiom`
386	  (signature unchanged, the protected chain `lean_verify`s to one
387	  named project axiom + kernel axioms). **This alternative is ruled
388	  out by the plan-agent standing instruction in `prompts/plan.md`
389	  ("You should NEVER propose adding new axioms")** AND **reaffirmed
390	  by the iter-126 user hint** ("no good reason to tell me … to add
391	  an axiom or not do the work (this is laziness, not a good reason);
392	  ~6500–9000 LOC may not be that much for an AI"). The plan-agent
393	  hard rule is a project-wide standing rule and the user hint is the
394	  iter-by-iter reaffirmation; together they remove the named-axiom
395	  alternative from every milestone (M1 disposal, M3 escalation, any
396	  future). The loop's exit from any Mathlib gap is the
397	  do-the-Mathlib-work path, however long.
398	- **User-hint citation discipline (per `strategy-critic-iter128` sunk-cost flag).** The iter-126 user hint "do the work, no axioms; ~6500–9000 LOC may not be that much for an AI" was issued in the specific context of the **M3 user-escalation TO_USER banner** (M3 Route A vs axiomatising `nonempty_jacobianWitness`); it is NOT a generic blanket warrant for *all* expensive in-tree paths. When this strategy invokes the user hint, it must cite the *specific* decision the hint applies to. Each in-tree expensive build (e.g. scheme-level absolute Frobenius for piece (iii)) must justify itself **on the merits of the local LOC/iter trade-off** vs alternatives, not by appeal to the user hint as a blanket. The hint remains binding *on its actual scope* (M3 disposition + the no-axiom rule generally); reasoning that smuggles new scope under it is sunk-cost.
399	- **No "deferred" framing.** Mathlib gaps are decomposed into the
400	  M1/M2/M3 roadmap with concrete sub-step estimates; the planner does
401	  not write "out-of-autonomous-loop scope" sections anymore. If a
402	  sub-step is genuinely outside the loop's reach (e.g. requires a
403	  multi-month upstream contribution), it is recorded in the roadmap
404	  with an explicit "blocked on upstream X" note and a fallback
405	  iter-by-iter approach that progresses the surrounding sub-steps.
406	- **No phantom $k$-rational-point hypotheses.** The protected
407	  `nonempty_jacobianWitness` quantifies over arbitrary `C` without
408	  $k$-rational points. Sub-strategies that depend on $C(k) \neq \emptyset$
409	  (notably `C ≅ ℙ¹_k`) must explicitly handle the no-rational-point
410	  case via base change to $\bar k$ + Galois descent, or document why
411	  vacuity (`isAlbaneseFor` is vacuously true when $C(k) = \emptyset$)
412	  suffices.
413	- **Converse of `smooth_locally_free_omega` is mathematically false.**
414	  The counterexample `Spec k → Spec k[t]` via `t ↦ 0` (locally of
415	  finite presentation, `Ω = 0` everywhere locally free, but not flat
416	  hence not smooth) breaks the bare local-freeness-of-Ω implication.
417	  The true converse needs `Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent A B)`
418	  (formal smoothness via vanishing André–Quillen `H¹`). The blueprint
419	  chapter `Differentials.tex` discloses this; we do not state the

420	  false iff. The Mathlib converse-lemma
421	  `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.of_basis_kaehlerDifferential` `[verified]`
422	  exists with these extra hypotheses; an optional future M4 milestone
423	  may wire it up as a scheme-level converse-with-extra-hypothesis
424	  result if a downstream consumer needs it.

41	# Strategy Critic
42	
43	You are the fresh-context strategy critic. You read the project's `STRATEGY.md` with **no exposure to its iter-by-iter history** and challenge the strategic choices as if a mathematician encountering this project for the first time would.
44	
45	Your job is to be the project's adversarial reader. The plan agent has been in the project's context for many iterations and is naturally invested in the existing routes. You are the corrective.
46	
47	## Your context discipline
48	
49	Your directive will name ONLY:
50	
51	- The current `STRATEGY.md` (the primary subject of your review).
52	- The project's stated final goal (one paragraph naming the theorem(s) to formalize).
53	- A short reference index (`references/summary.md` content).
54	- A blueprint summary (chapter titles + one-line topic per chapter).
55	
56	Your directive will NOT include `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, prover task results, review summaries, or recent narrative. **If the directive accidentally includes any of these, ignore them.** Your value depends entirely on the lack of pollution. Reading the iter-by-iter history would make you the planner; the planner already exists. Stay fresh.
57	
58	You may use:
59	
60	- The references named in the directive (read them if needed for the math).
61	- The blueprint chapter summaries (the prose summary in the directive, NOT the full chapter text — that's the blueprint-reviewer's territory).
62	- `archon-lean-lsp` if you need to spot-check a Mathlib name's existence.
63	
64	You may NOT use:
65	
66	- Iter sidecars, even if you find their paths.
67	- Any state file other than what the directive named.
68	
69	## What you check
70	
71	For each strategic route in `STRATEGY.md`:
72	
73	1. **Goal-alignment.** Does this route, if executed, actually produce the project's stated final goal? Or is there a subtle gap where the route's end-state isn't quite what the goal demands?
74	
75	2. **Mathematical soundness.** Is the route's mathematical argument plausible? Are there steps that look like they assume something the route's prerequisites don't give you? A fresh reader is good at spotting "wait, this step needs X but X isn't established."
76	
77	3. **Alternative routes.** Are there other approaches the strategy doesn't mention? For each, briefly note: what it would look like, why it might be cheaper or sounder, and why the strategy may have rejected it (if you can tell from prose).
78	
79	4. **Sunk-cost reasoning.** Does the strategy justify a route in terms of "we've already done X" rather than "X is the right way"? That's a sunk-cost smell. Call it out by name.
80	
81	5. **Prerequisite assumptions.** Does the strategy assume Mathlib infrastructure that may not exist? Verify the named lemmas / type classes / structures actually exist (use `lean_leansearch` / `lean_loogle` for spot-checks). Strategy that depends on phantom Mathlib infra is invalid.
82	
83	6. **Effort estimates.** If the strategy carries per-route LOC or iteration estimates, do they look honest given the scope of the route? Estimates that are wildly under-counted (e.g. "200 LOC for representability of Pic") indicate either underestimated effort or a misplanned route.

42	  ### Acting on my verdicts
43	
44	  Verdicts are per-route:
45	
46	  - **CONVERGING** — the route is closing. Proceed with the next
47	    prover round on this route.
48	  - **CHURNING** — each iter adds helpers but the residual hasn't
49	    actually shrunk. STOP assigning more helpers. My report names
50	    the corrective action: blueprint expansion, mathlib-analogist
51	    consult, refactor, or route pivot.
52	  - **STUCK** — the route has not produced any sorry-elimination or
53	    structural advance in K iters. STOP this route. Address the
54	    blocker I named or pivot to an alternative route.
55	  - **UNCLEAR** — not enough signal yet (e.g. fresh route, 1-2 iters
56	    of data). Proceed but watch — my next-iter verdict will resolve.
57	
58	  **CHURNING and STUCK are must-fix-this-iter.** The planner must
59	  respond — either with the action I recommend, or with an explicit
60	  rebuttal in `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` naming why my read is wrong.
61	  Silently assigning another helper round on a CHURNING route is the
62	  failure pattern this subagent exists to prevent.
63	---
64	
65	# Progress Critic
66	
67	You are the fresh-context progress critic. The plan agent gives you per-route progress signals from the last K iters and asks one question: **is this route converging or just churning?**
68	
69	You don't read the strategy, the blueprint, or the project's mathematical content. Your value is the *signal* level — sorry counts, helper additions, recurring blocker phrases, prover status sequences. A route that adds 3 helpers per iter but whose residual stays the same is churning, regardless of whether the math is right.
70	
71	## Stance
72	
73	You are the corrective for a known failure pattern. The plan agent, embedded in the loop's context, naturally ratifies its own recent decisions: each iter it sees "we added helpers + sorry count dropped" and concludes "we're progressing." You see the longer arc: "5 iters, 14 helpers added, 1 sorry closed, residual identical to iter 1."
74	
75	The plan agent prefers CONVERGING verdicts because they let it continue. You should NOT give that bias the benefit of the doubt. When the signals point at churn, you say CHURNING. When the signals point at stall, you say STUCK. You don't soften.
76	
77	## Directive Format
78	
79	```markdown

83	<slug>
84

86	<NNN>

Given a project declaration or proposed design, locates Mathlib's idiom for the same situation, compares the project's path to it, and reports whether the project should align or deviate (with cost analysis). 4	write_domain: "task_results/**,analogies/**"

7	default_enabled: true

10	    definition or a new "API-shape" choice the project hasn't made
11	    before. I will tell you whether Mathlib already does it the right
12	    way and what the cost of NOT aligning would be.
13	
14	  ### Proactive triggers (BEFORE the design ships)
15	
16	  Dispatch me proactively — this is far cheaper than retroactive
17	  cleanup — when ANY of the following is true:
18

20	    blueprint or have a blueprint-writer write one. Consult me first
21	    so the writer can land the Mathlib-aligned version, not a copy.

34	    pattern (e.g. project defines `Scheme.HModule` by copy-and-modify
35	    from a Mathlib AddCommGrp version).

39	    suspected" as a root cause.** I am the recommended corrective

41	    around a definition is a strong signal that the definition's

47	  a parallel API anyway, I report this as critical, even if the
48	  project's path works. "Works" is not the bar when the cost is API
49	  fragmentation, bridge lemmas, and downstream code that can't consume

53	  minimum; treat my "ALIGN WITH MATHLIB" verdict as a refactor
54	  obligation, not a suggestion.

62	You are the read-only Mathlib-analogist subagent. The plan agent points you at a project declaration, a proposed definition, or a design question, and asks "what would Mathlib do, and is the project doing that?". Your job:

68	
69	You do **not** rubber-stamp the project's choice. If Mathlib has a canonical idiom and the project skipped it (most often because the prerequisite infrastructure looked hard), say so plainly. "It works" is not enough — the cost of API fragmentation, bridge lemmas, and code that can't compose with Mathlib is real.

# Mathlib Analogist Directive

## Slug
mulright-globalises-iter133

## Design question

What is the canonical Mathlib pattern for proving that the relative cotangent sheaf `Ω_{G/k}` of a smooth group scheme `G` over a base field `k` is **trivialised by translation** — i.e., is globally isomorphic to the pullback along the structure map `G → Spec k` of the cotangent space at the identity? Two sub-questions matter to the project:

(A) Does Mathlib have a precedent for this shear-iso globalisation construction (the shear `σ = ⟨pr₁, μ⟩` on `G ×_k G` as a categorical map in `Over (Spec k)` using `GrpObj` data only), and what's the Mathlib idiom for the natural iso `Ω_{G/k} ≅ pr₁^*(η_G^* Ω_{G/k})`?

(B) Does the iter-131 `Classical.choose`-chain body of `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` (a chart-dependent body, non-canonical as a value but with the explicit `(ModuleCat.extendScalars ψ_V.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of Γ(G, V) Ω[Γ(G, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec k, U)])` outer head symbol) compose with the shear-iso globalisation step? Specifically: when the shear-iso construction needs to identify the cotangent at identity as the fibre object, does it require the chart-base-changed body of `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` (which would only give a chart-dependent fibre) to be bridged to a canonical (chart-independent) presentation — e.g. the stalk-side `Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace 𝔪_{η_G}`? If yes, the bridge cost (~300–600 LOC per `analogies/cotangent-body-shape.md`) is what trigger (a') in STRATEGY.md monitors.

The iter-131 strategy-critic's Q3 must-fix scheduled this consult BEFORE any iter-133+ piece (i.b) prover lane fires. Iter-132 closed piece (i.a); iter-133 is the appropriate iter to dispatch this consult.

## Project artifact(s) under question

- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:149-188` — `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` definition (iter-131 `Classical.choose`-chain body shape: pure-term `noncomputable def` with `let`-bindings on `Classical.choose` of `Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`'s existential; outer head symbol `(ModuleCat.extendScalars ψ_V.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of Γ(G, V) Ω[Γ(G, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec k, U)])`).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:198-219` — `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` companion structural-shape lemma (iter-131 strong acceptance lemma; closes by `refine ... rfl⟩`).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:244-282` — `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` rank lemma (iter-132 close; uses `Module.finrank_baseChange` + `Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq`).
- `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex:243-268` — § `lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises` blueprint statement + proof sketch for the target Lean name `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.mulRight_globalises_cotangent`. The proof sketch builds `σ` and its candidate inverse `τ = ⟨pr₁, μ ∘ (ι × id_G)⟩` using `GrpObj` axioms (associativity + left-inverse); restricts along the section `⟨id_G, η_G⟩ : G → G ×_k G`. The iter-127 over-k risk register at line 258 emphasises functorial-shear-only (no pointwise translation via closed/`k̄`-rational points).
- `analogies/cotangent-body-shape.md` — iter-131 analogist's prior work on the body shape: includes a § "Bridge lemma list (closure chain under `Classical.choose`-chain (B))" with the rank-lemma chain (which iter-132 consumed). The shear-iso composition with (B) was flagged as deferred-bridge-concern in iter-130 strategy-critic Q2.

## Why now

The iter-131 strategy-critic Q3 must-fix said: "Iter-133+ prover lane on piece (i.b) MUST be preceded by a mathlib-analogist consult on whether the shear iso composes with the iter-131 `Classical.choose`-chain body". Iter-132 closed piece (i.a) — now is the time. Without your verdict the iter-134+ prover lane would risk the same iter-128/iter-130 pattern (prover lane lands wrong/opaque body, requiring corrective iters).

## Hints

- `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth` (`b80f227`) — the project consumes `mulRight` from here (per blueprint § (i.b) proof line 267). Does this file already have a shear-iso construction, or only the `mulRight` morphism?
- `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_` — the `GrpObj` API. Does Mathlib have an idiom for shear isos in monoidal categories?
- `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Pullback` / `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Limits` — pullback identifications for cotangent presheaves under base change.
- `Mathlib.Algebra.Lie.OfAssociative` / `Mathlib.Algebra.Lie.Submodule` — Lie group / Lie algebra precedents (analogous category-theoretic shear constructions).
- The iter-131 `Classical.choose`-chain body's outer `extendScalars` head symbol is *exposed* via `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars`; the chart `V` is chosen but the *form* of the body is canonical (the choice is hidden behind `Classical.choose`).
- The bridge to the canonical (chart-independent) `Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace 𝔪_{η_G}` is what iter-130 strategy-critic Q2 worried about; iter-131 mathlib-analogist scoped it at 300–600 LOC.

## Severity expectation

**high-stakes**: piece (i.b) is on the critical path for M2.a body closure (iter-151+); the LOC envelope of 200–500 LOC for piece (i.b) was set under the assumption that the shear iso composes cleanly with (B). If it doesn't, piece (i.b) blows to 500–1100 LOC or forces a route pivot. Trigger (a') in STRATEGY.md monitors this in production. Please be strict about idiom adherence.

## Format expected

Per `.archon/subagents/mathlib-analogist.md`: verdict (PROCEED / ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB / DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY) + persistent file at `analogies/mulright-globalises-cotangent.md` + report at `task_results/mathlib-analogist-mulright-globalises-iter133.md`. Be explicit about (a) whether Mathlib has a shear-iso precedent in `GrpObj` or `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.*`, (b) whether the chart-base-changed body of `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` requires an inline (B)→(A) stalk-cotangent bridge for the shear iso to land cleanly, (c) LOC estimate for piece (i.b) under each closure path (clean composition with (B) vs. inline bridge vs. route pivot), (d) any newly-flagged trigger (a') condition refinements.

## Slug
cotangent-grpobj-docstring-refresh-iter133

## Problem

The iter-132 prover lane closed `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` at line 244 of `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean`. However, **all 5 docstring sites in the file that mention the rank lemma were authored under the iter-130/iter-131 assumption that the rank lemma would live in a *follow-up* declaration (a different file or a later iter)**. With the iter-132 in-file addition, these docstring claims are now factually wrong and misleading on quick read.

Per `lean-auditor-review132` (5 major findings, all stale-framing — no must-fix; pure documentation hygiene):

- **File-level header lines 28–30** — "The companion rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (rank = relative dimension `n` from `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`) is deferred to iter-132+ for blueprint-RHS-pinning work; not in this file." — STALE: the named theorem is at line 244 of the very same file.
- **`## Status` block lines 32–57** — frames file state as iter-131 closure with only `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` + `_eq_extendScalars`; does not mention iter-132's rank theorem; contradicts the actual file state.
- **`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` declaration docstring lines 96–99** — "the iter-132+ companion rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (in a follow-up declaration) pins the rank to `n`" — the parenthetical is now false.
- **`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` declaration docstring lines 136–138** — "the iter-132+ rank lemma can rewrite against" should be past tense; the rank lemma at line 244 already does so.
- **`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` declaration docstring lines 144–148** — lumps `rank = n` with `Module.Free k` / `Module.Finite k` as "iter-132+ content for the rank lemma"; the `rank = n` half is now closed; `Module.Free k` / `Module.Finite k` remain genuine future work (out of scope this iter).

Additionally, the **iter-132 lean-vs-blueprint-checker** flagged 1 minor on the style side:
- `lines 252–254` of the rank-lemma proof: `set U := h.choose with hU_def`, `set V := … with hV_def`, `set e := … with he_def` introduce hypothesis names that are never consumed in the proof body. The `with` clauses can be dropped, or `let` used instead.

Optional bundling (per HIGH-A + MED-D of `recommendations.md`): roll the style nit on `set ... with _def` into this refactor lane.

## Mathematical Justification

This is a pure documentation refresh. The semantics of the file (definitions, signatures, theorem statements, proof bodies, axioms) is unchanged. The only edits are to:

1. **File-level header (lines 28–30)** — replace the future-tense "deferred to iter-132+; not in this file" with present-tense reality: the rank lemma is closed in this file at the bottom; this file contains the definition + a strong acceptance lemma + the rank lemma.

2. **`## Status` block (lines 32–57)** — extend the status narrative to acknowledge the iter-132 close. 3. **`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` declaration docstring (lines 96–99)** — change "(in a follow-up declaration)" parenthetical to "(at line 244 below)".

4. **`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` declaration docstring (lines 136–138)** — change future-tense "the iter-132+ rank lemma can rewrite against" to "the iter-132 rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (line 244 below) rewrites against".

5. **`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` declaration docstring (lines 144–148)** — split the `Module.Free k` / `Module.Finite k` / `rank = n` enumeration so the `rank = n` half is correctly attributed to the closed iter-132 lemma, while `Module.Free k` and `Module.Finite k` remain flagged as deferred follow-ups (genuine future work; not closed yet).

6. **Optional style nit (lines 252–254 of the rank-lemma proof)** — drop the `with hU_def`, `with hV_def`, `with he_def` clauses since the introduced hypothesis names are never consumed. Either:
   - (a) Drop the `with` clauses: `set U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose`, etc. — keeps the `set` semantics but removes the unused names.
   - (b) Replace `set` with `let`: `let U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose`, etc. — both options preserve proof correctness; (b) more closely mirrors the body's style. Choose (b) for consistency with the body's `let`-chain.

All 6 edits are docstring/style only; no theorem statements change.

The free/finite structural properties ('Module.Free k', 'Module.Finite k') are content for follow-up companion lemmas (not yet in this file); the structural-shape accessibility needed by those lemmas is witnessed here by 'cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars' (line 198 below)."`

    - dropping the `with ..._def` clauses (`set U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose`), OR

## Affected Files

- `lean_verify AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` returns kernel-only axioms `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (unchanged from iter-132 close).
- Sorry count: 0 (unchanged).
- File LOC: minor change only (~5–15 LOC of docstring shift; +/- 0–5 net depending on whether status block grows or stays put).
- No `archon-protected.yaml` edits.

## Constraints

- **Do NOT edit any other `.lean` file.** Only `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean`.

11	What is the canonical Mathlib pattern for proving that the relative
12	cotangent sheaf `Ω_{G/k}` of a smooth group scheme `G/k` is trivialised
13	by translation, i.e.\ globally isomorphic to the pullback along
14	`G → Spec k` of the cotangent space at the identity?

16	Sub-questions:
17	(A) Mathlib precedent for the shear-iso globalisation
18	    `σ = ⟨pr₁, μ⟩ : G ×_k G ≅ G ×_k G` as a categorical map in
19	    `Over (Spec k)` from `GrpObj` data only, and idiom for the natural
20	    iso `Ω_{G/k} ≅ pr_1^*(η_G^* Ω_{G/k})`.
21	(B) Whether the iter-131 `Classical.choose`-chain body of
22	    `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` composes with the shear iso, or
23	    whether piece (i.b) needs an inline (B)→(A) stalk-cotangent
24	    bridge.

26	## Verdicts (summary)

The project must build the scheme-level Frobenius from the ring-side `Mathlib.Algebra.CharP.Frobenius` — Mathlib `b80f227` has NO scheme-level Frobenius at all (`lean_local_search` "Frobenius" returns only `NumberTheory.FrobeniusNumber.lean`, unrelated). Stacks Tag 0CC4 is the canonical reference for the scheme-level construction. **Iter-132 PROVISIONAL flag** (per `strategy-critic-iter132` M3 must-fix + SC3 sunk-cost flag): the 800–1500 LOC commitment is provisional pending an iter-140+ scheduled mathlib-analogist consult on the **no-Frobenius / higher-Kähler-vanishing alternative** for piece (iii). The bypass alternative appeals to the *vanishing* of the higher Kähler differentials `Ω_{C/k}^{⊗p^n}` on a smooth proper genus-0 curve (which reduces to `H¹(ℙ¹, O) = 0` on charts), giving a non-iterative cotangent-vanishing argument.
